sections.label: Section 1034
sections.original: οὐδʼ αὖ τοσοῦτον ἔχθος ἐχθαίρω σʼ ἐγώ .
sections.translation: But , again , I do not hate you with so great a hate .

sections.label: Section 1035
sections.original: ἀλλʼ οὖν ἐπίστω γʼ οἷ μʼ ἀτιμίας ἄγεις .
sections.translation: Yet know at least to what dishonor you drive me .

sections.label: Section 1036
sections.original: ἀτιμίας μὲν οὔ , προμηθίας δὲ σοῦ .
sections.translation: Dishonor , no ! It is forethought for you .

sections.label: Section 1037
sections.original: τῷ σῷ δικαίῳ δῆτʼ ἐπισπέσθαι με δεῖ ;
sections.translation: Am I bound , then , to follow your rule of right ?

sections.label: Section 1038
sections.original: ὅταν γὰρ εὖ φρονῇς , τόθʼ ἡγήσει σὺ νῷν .
sections.translation: Yes , for when you are sensible , then at that time you shall lead the both of us .

sections.label: Section 1039
sections.original: ἦ δεινὸν εὖ λέγουσαν ἐξαμαρτάνειν .
sections.translation: How terrible it is that one who speaks so well should be so wrong !

sections.label: Section 1040
sections.original: εἴρηκας ὀρθῶς ᾧ σὺ πρόσκεισαι κακῷ .
sections.translation: You have well described the fault to which you devotedly cling .

sections.label: Section 1041
sections.original: τί δʼ ; οὐ δοκῶ σοι ταῦτα σὺν δίκῃ λέγειν ;
sections.translation: What ? Do you think that my words are not the words of Justice ?

sections.label: Section 1042
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἔστιν ἔνθα χἠ δίκη βλάβην φέρει .
sections.translation: But sometimes even Justice herself causes harm .

sections.label: Section 1043
sections.original: τούτοις ἐγὼ ζῆν τοῖς νόμοις οὐ βούλομαι .
sections.translation: I do not care to live where that attitude is lawful .

sections.label: Section 1044
sections.original: ἀλλʼ εἰ ποήσεις ταῦτʼ , ἐπαινέσεις ἐμέ .
sections.translation: Well , if you must do this , you will commend me yet .

sections.label: Section 1045
sections.original: καὶ μὴν ποήσω γʼ οὐδὲν ἐκπλαγεῖσά σε .
sections.translation: And do it I will , not a bit disturbed by you .

sections.label: Section 1046
sections.original: καὶ τοῦτʼ ἀληθές , οὐδὲ βουλεύσει πάλιν ;
sections.translation: Is this true ? You will not reconsider your plan ?

sections.label: Section 1047
sections.original: βουλῆς γὰρ οὐδέν ἐστιν ἔχθιον κακῆς .
sections.translation: No , for no enemy is more damaging than bad advice .

sections.label: Section 1048
sections.original: φρονεῖν ἔοικας οὐδὲν ὧν ἐγὼ λέγω .
sections.translation: You seem to agree with nothing that I say .

sections.label: Section 1049
sections.original: πάλαι δέδοκται ταῦτα κοὐ νεωστί μοι .
sections.translation: My resolve is not new , but long since fixed .

sections.label: Section 1050
sections.original: ἄπειμι τοίνυν · οὔτε γὰρ σὺ τἄμʼ ἔπη
sections.translation: Then I will go . You cannot be brought to approve my words , nor I your conduct .

sections.label: Section 1052
sections.original: ἀλλʼ εἴσιθʼ . οὔ σοι μὴ μεθέψομαί ποτε ,
sections.translation: No , go inside . I will never come after you , even though you may strongly desire it , since it is great folly even to attempt a useless quest .

sections.label: Section 1055
sections.original: ἀλλʼ εἰ σεαυτῇ τυγχάνεις δοκοῦσά τι
sections.translation: Well , if you seem to think straight in your own eyes , may you go on thinking so . Eventually , when you have fallen into trouble , you will approve my advice . Exit Chrysothemis into the house .

sections.label: Section 1.1060
sections.original: τί τοὺς ἄνωθεν φρονιμωτάτους οἰωνοὺς ἐσορώμενοι τροφᾶς
sections.translation: of those from whom they derived life and enjoyment , why do we not pay these debts in like measure ? No , by the lightning-flash of Zeus , by Themis throned in the sky ,

sections.label: Section 1.1065
sections.original: δαρὸν οὐκ ἀπόνητοι .
sections.translation: we are not long unpunished . O Voice of the underworld that reaches to mortals , shout for me a piteous cry to the sons of Atreus below . Carry the reproaches not appropriate to my dancing !

sections.label: Section 1.1070
sections.original: ὅτι σφὶν ἤδη τὰ μὲν ἐκ δόμων νοσεῖ δή , τὰ δὲ πρὸς τέκνων διπλῆ
sections.translation: Tell them the affairs of their house , how it is now diseased ; how among his children , double-sided strife has overwhelmed their loving manner .

sections.label: Section 1.1075
sections.original: Ἠλέκτρα , τὸν ἀεὶ πατρὸς
sections.translation: Electra , betrayed , braves the storm alone . In misery she bewails her father ’ s fate without pause , like the all-grieving nightingale . She cares not at all about death , but is ready for that eternal blindness ,

sections.label: Section 1.1080
sections.original: διδύμαν ἑλοῦσʼ Ἐρινύν · τίς ἂν εὔπατρις ὧδε βλάστοι ;
sections.translation: could she but subdue the double Erinys of her house . Who could grow to be so noble a daughter of so noble a father ?

sections.label: Section 2.1082
sections.original: ζῶν κακῶς εὔκλειαν αἰσχῦναι θέλει
sections.translation: None of the good willingly clouds his fair repute and becomes nameless by leading a corrupt life , my child .

sections.label: Section 2.1085
sections.original: ὡς καὶ σὺ πάγκλαυτον αἰῶνα κοινὸν εἵλου ,
sections.translation: Similarly , you , too , have chosen a lifetime of shared mourning and have armed against dishonor , so that you might win in one breath a twofold praise as wise , and as the best of daughters .

sections.label: Section 2.1090
sections.original: ζῴης μοι καθύπερθεν
sections.translation: May I yet see you live exalted in might and wealth above your enemies by as much as you now dwell beneath their hand ! For I have found you enjoying no prosperous estate , yet

sections.label: Section 2.1095
sections.original: βεβῶσαν , ἃ δὲ μέγιστʼ ἔβλαστε νόμιμα , τῶνδε φερομέναν
sections.translation: for observance of nature ’ s highest laws you win the noblest prize by your reverence toward Zeus .

sections.label: Section 1098
sections.original: ὀρθῶς θʼ ὁδοιποροῦμεν ἔνθα χρῄζομεν ;
sections.translation: Ladies , have we been directed aright , and are we on the right path to our goal ?

sections.label: Section 1100
sections.original: τί δʼ ἐξερευνᾷς καὶ τί βουληθεὶς πάρει ;
sections.translation: What do you seek ? What desire brings you here ?

sections.label: Section 1101
sections.original: Αἴγισθον ἔνθʼ ᾤκηκεν ἱστορῶ πάλαι .
sections.translation: I have long been searching for the home of Aegisthus .

sections.label: Section 1102
sections.original: ἀλλʼ εὖ θʼ ἱκάνεις χὠ φράσας ἀζήμιος .
sections.translation: Well , you have found it , and your guide is blameless .

sections.label: Section 1103
sections.original: τίς οὖν ἂν ὑμῶν τοῖς ἔσω φράσειεν ἂν
sections.translation: Which of you , then , would tell those inside of the long-desired presence of us travelers ?

sections.label: Section 1105
sections.original: ἥδʼ , εἰ τὸν ἄγχιστόν γε κηρύσσειν χρεών .
sections.translation: She will , if the nearest in kin should announce it .

sections.label: Section 1106
sections.original: ἴθʼ , ὦ γύναι , δήλωσον εἰσελθοῦσʼ ὅτι
sections.translation: Go , lady , enter and make it known that certain men of Phocis seek Aegisthus .

sections.label: Section 1108
sections.original: οἴμοι τάλαινʼ , οὐ δή ποθʼ ἧς ἠκούσαμεν
sections.translation: Ah , miserable me ! Surely you do not bring proof positive of that rumor which we heard ?

sections.label: Section 1110
sections.original: οὐκ οἶδα τὴν σὴν κληδόνʼ · ἀλλά μοι γέρων
sections.translation: I know nothing of your rumor ; but the aged Strophius ordered me to give report of Orestes .

sections.label: Section 1112
sections.original: τί δʼ ἔστιν , ὦ ξένʼ ; ὥς μʼ ὑπέρχεται φόβος .
sections.translation: What is it , sir ? Ah , how fear creeps over me !

sections.label: Section 1113
sections.original: φέροντες αὐτοῦ σμικρὰ λείψανʼ ἐν βραχεῖ
sections.translation: We come bearing his scanty remains in a small urn , as you see .

sections.label: Section 1115
sections.original: οἲ ʼγὼ τάλαινα , τοῦτʼ ἐκεῖνʼ ἤδη σαφὲς
sections.translation: Oh , the misery ! Here , at last , my eyes look for certain , it seems , upon that grievous burden in your hand .

sections.label: Section 1117
sections.original: εἴπερ τι κλαίεις τῶν Ὀρεστείων κακῶν ,
sections.translation: If your tears are for any of Orestes ’ tribulations , know that this vessel is his body ’ s home .

sections.label: Section 1119
sections.original: ὦ ξεῖνε , δός νυν , πρὸς θεῶν , εἴπερ τόδε
sections.translation: Ah , sir , if this urn indeed contains him , then allow me ,

sections.label: Section 1120
sections.original: κέκευθεν αὐτὸν τεῦχος , εἰς χεῖρας λαβεῖν ,
sections.translation: by the gods , to take it in my hands , so that I may weep and wail , not for these ashes alone , but for myself and for all our house with them !

sections.label: Section 1123
sections.original: δόθʼ , ἥτις ἐστί , προσφέροντες · οὐ γὰρ ὡς
sections.translation: Take it and give it to her , whoever she may be . For she asks this for herself not as if with hostile intent ,

sections.label: Section 1125
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἢ φίλων τις ἢ πρὸς αἵματος φύσιν .
sections.translation: but like one who is his friend , or a kinswoman by blood . The urn is placed in Electra ’ s hands .

sections.label: Section 1126
sections.original: ὦ φιλτάτου μνημεῖον ἀνθρώπων ἐμοὶ
sections.translation: Memorial of him whom I loved best on earth , sole remnant of Orestes ’ vitality ! How contrary to the hopes with which I sent you away do I receive you back ! Now I raise your nothingness in my hands ;

sections.label: Section 1130
sections.original: δόμων δέ σʼ , ὦ παῖ , λαμπρὸν ἐξέπεμψʼ ἐγώ .
sections.translation: but then , my child , you were radiant , when I sent you away from home ! Would that I had first abandoned life , before , stealing you away with these hands , I sent you to a strange land and rescued you from death , in order that you might have lain dead on that same day

sections.label: Section 1135
sections.original: τύμβου πατρῴου κοινὸν εἰληχὼς μέρος .
sections.translation:
and had your share in the tomb of our father !
But now , an exile from home and fatherland , you have perished miserably , far from your sister . Ah , me , these loving hands have not washed or decked your corpse , nor taken up

sections.label: Section 1140
sections.original: ἀνειλόμην , ὡς εἰκός , ἄθλιον βάρος ,
sections.translation:
their sad burden from the all-consuming pyre , as was proper . No ! At the hands of strangers , poor Orestes , you have been tended , and so have come to us , a small bulk in a small urn .
Ah , I grieve at the uselessness of my nursing long ago , the service that I often bestowed

sections.label: Section 1145
sections.original: πόνῳ γλυκεῖ παρέσχον · οὔτε γάρ ποτε
sections.translation: on you in sweet labor ! For you were never your mother ’ s darling so much as mine , nor was any in the house your nurse but I , and by you I was ever called sister . But now all this has vanished in a day

sections.label: Section 1150
sections.original: θανόντι σὺν σοί · πάντα γὰρ συναρπάσας
sections.translation: with your death . Like a whirlwind you have swept everything away with you . Our father is gone ; I am dead because of you ; you yourself are dead and gone ; our enemies laugh at us ; and our mother , who is no mother , raves with joy . Unknown to her , you often

sections.label: Section 1155
sections.original: φήμας λάθρᾳ προύπεμπες ὡς φανούμενος
sections.translation: sent me messages about her , saying that you yourself would appear for vengeance . But our evil fortune , yours and mine , has torn all that away , and has sent you back to me in this state , ash and a useless shade in place of your beloved form .

sections.label: Section 1160
sections.original: οἴμοι μοι .
sections.translation: ah , me , ah , me ! O pitiable body ! Alas , dear one sent on a most dire journey , how you have destroyed me , destroyed me indeed , my brother !

sections.label: Section 1165
sections.original: τοιγὰρ σὺ δέξαι μʼ ἐς τὸ σὸν τόδε στέγος ,
sections.translation: Therefore accept me into this abode of yours — me , a nothing , into your nothingness ,— so that I may dwell with you hereafter below . For when you were on earth , we shared equally , and now I wish to die and not to be left out of your grave ,

sections.label: Section 1170
sections.original: τοὺς γὰρ θανόντας οὐχ ὁρῶ λυπουμένους .
sections.translation: since I see that the dead are relieved of pain .

sections.label: Section 1171
sections.original: θνητοῦ πέφυκας πατρός , Ἠλέκτρα , φρόνει ,
sections.translation: Remember , Electra , you are the child of a mortal father , and Orestes was mortal . Therefore do not grieve too much . Death is a debt which all of us must pay .

sections.label: Section 1174
sections.original: φεῦ φεῦ . τί λέξω ; ποῖ λόγων ἀμηχανῶν
sections.translation: Ah , what shall I say ? I am at a loss . To what words

sections.label: Section 1175
sections.original: ἔλθω ; κρατεῖν γὰρ οὐκέτι γλώσσης σθένω .
sections.translation: can I turn ? I no longer have the strength to master my tongue !

sections.label: Section 1176
sections.original: τί δʼ ἔσχες ἄλγος ; πρὸς τί τοῦτʼ εἰπὼν κυρεῖς ;
sections.translation: What has troubled you ? Why did you say that ?

sections.label: Section 1177
sections.original: ἦ σὸν τὸ κλεινὸν εἶδος Ἠλέκτρας τόδε ;
sections.translation: Is this the illustrious form of Electra ?
